sourceList.title: Johann Jacob im Eintrag für Johann Jacob, „Deutschland Heiraten, 1558-1929“

nameMeanings.name: Jacob
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish, English, Welsh, German, Portuguese, French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, Swedish, Danish, and Norwegian: derivative, via Latin Jacob(us), from the Hebrew personal name Ya‘aqob (Yaakov). In the Bible, this is the name of the crafty younger twin brother of Esau (Genesis 25:26), who took advantage of the latter's hunger and impetuousness to persuade him to part with his birthright ‘for a mess of potage’. The name is traditionally interpreted as coming from Hebrew akev ‘heel’: Jacob is said to have been born holding on to Esau's heel. In English usage the name Jacob is regarded as distinct from the name James , but they are of identical origin. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Yaqub , Yakub , Yacoub , or Yacob , Slovenian Jakob and Jakop, Czech and Slovak Jakub , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Jacobs and Jacobson ). The name Jacob is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Chacko ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Chacko
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Oommen, Varughese, Eapen, Babu, Mathai, Ninan, Cheriyan, Shanti, Aleykutty, Biji, Harsha, Jaya.

Indian (Kerala) and Jewish (from India): from a vernacular pet form of the personal name   Jacob  , found among Christians in southern India and Jews of Cochin. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Americanized form of Slovak Čačko: nickname from čačka ‘toy, plaything’.

variants.name: James
meanings: English and Welsh: from the Middle English personal name James. Introduced to England by the Normans, this is an Old French form of Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Latin Iacobus, Greek Iakōbos, the New Testament rendering of Hebrew Ya‘aqob (see Jacob ). The medieval Latin (Vulgate) Bible distinguished between Old Testament Iacob (which was uninflected) and New Testament Iacobus (with inflections). The latter developed into James in medieval French. The distinction was carried over into the King James Bible of 1611, and Jacob and James remain as separate names in English usage. Most European languages, however, make no such distinction, so that forms such as French Jacques , stand for both the Old and the New Testament names. This surname is also very common among African Americans. Compare Jack .

variants.name: Coppin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Camille, Christophe.
English and French: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Copin, a pet form of Jacob .
English: variant of   Copping  .

variants.name: Agopian
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Armenian Agop, Raffi, Aram, Arshag, Bedros, Dikran, Harut, Kaloust, Kevork, Melkon, Noubar, Nubar.
Armenian: patronymic from the personal name Agop, a variant of Hagop, western Armenian form of Jacob . Compare Hagopian and Akopyan .

variants.name: Akopyan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Armenian Aykanush, Grigor, Oganes, Akop, Andranik, Araksya, Aram, Ararat, Knarik, Mais, Sarkis, Tadevos.  Russian Asya, Georgy, Grigory, Grisha, Mikhail, Sergey, Valeriy, Vladimir, Yury.

Armenian: patronymic from the personal name Akop, Russianized form of Hakob, eastern Armenian form of Jacob . This form (transliteration) of the surname is found mainly in Russia. Compare Agopian , Hakobyan , and Hakopian .

variants.name: Coppi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Dino, Gino, Renzo.
Italian: from the personal name Coppo, a short form ofIacoppo (see Jacob ).

variants.name: Bini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Mario, Aldo, Dario, Dino, Elisabetta, Franco, Graziano, Guido, Luciano, Nicolo, Romano.  French Achille, Collette.

Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Bino, a short form of any of various names ending in -bino, such as Iacobino (see Jacob ), Albino , Cherubino (see Cherubin ). This surname is also found in southern France, Lorraine, and Corsica.

variants.name: Covello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Giuseppe, Nunzio, Rocco, Angelo, Calogero, Carlo, Gaspare, Gasper, Geno, Gino, Gioacchino, Lia.

Italian (southern): from the personal name Covello, a diminutive of Iacovo, with loss of the first syllable. Iacovo is one of many old Italian forms of Latin Iacobus (see Jacob ), now preserved only in surnames.

variants.name: Cobey
meanings: Possibly also an Americanized form of French Cobet: from a shortened pet form of the personal name Jacob .

English (Sussex): variant of Cobby, perhaps a simple adjective formation in -y from Middle English cobbe, in a sense like ‘stout’ (hence ‘big, important’), or ‘lumpish’. Alternatively, perhaps a pet form of the Middle English personal name Cob(b) (see Cobb ).

variants.name: Buzzi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Julio, Carlo, Adriano, Angelina, Dario, Libero, Luigi, Mario, Renato, Riccardo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a short form of a personal name ending with -buzzo, for example Iacobuzzo, from Iacobo (see Jacob ).

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vernacular form of   Johannes .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob a eu au moins 1 fils.

it.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Johann Jacobは少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob(은)는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: У Johann Jacob был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
